name: AgentGit search value benchmark
description: A project-memory benchmark that checks whether AgentGit finds the commit context a new teammate or future agent would need.

queries.query: What is this project and how does AgentGit remember code decisions?
queries.why: A beginner asks for the original product plan and purpose.

queries.query: How is hybrid vector and keyword search implemented?
queries.why: A developer needs the commit that introduced search ranking.

queries.query: How do I show stored memory for HEAD or a short SHA?
queries.why: A user wants the command behavior for showing AgentCommit metadata.

queries.query: How can I visualize memory connections between commits?
queries.why: A user wants the graph view for related memories.

queries.query: What happens if memory storage fails after the git commit succeeds?
queries.why: A maintainer needs to understand pending memory recovery.

queries.query: What are the rules for agents before and after making code changes?
queries.why: A new agent needs the repository workflow instructions.

queries.query: How was the database setup made reliable for Supabase migrations?
queries.why: A developer is debugging setup and migration behavior.

queries.query: How do we backfill AgentGit memory from old git history?
queries.why: A maintainer wants to create memories for commits that happened before AgentGit was installed.

queries.query: How does AgentGit separate memories for different repositories?
queries.why: A team wants to avoid mixing memories from different projects.

queries.query: Why do agent commits have titles for graph labels?
queries.why: A developer wants to understand the graph labeling design.

queries.query: Where did the UI for browsing or backfilling agent commits come from?
queries.why: A teammate wants to find the product-facing UI work.
